description: §59 / §14.10 (Rx-6) — a bare `.Variant` in the VALUE position of a map method on a REACTIVE map cell resolves against the map's declared value type. `<m>: [string:Color]` then `@m = @m.insert("a", .Red)` and `@m.getOr("a", .Blue)` must compile CLEAN — before the fix the reactive-cell-map path lost the map's value-type context (the `mapType.value` threading) for bare-variant inference and false-fired E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS on `.Red` / `.Blue`. (The non-reactive LOCAL map read path was already clean; the real repro is the reactive-map value arg.) SCOPE: this case exercises ONLY the valid bare-variant path; a value typo (e.g. `.Rd`) is NOT tested here — at the markup event-handler locus that path is fail-open today, so no E-TYPE-063 claim is made.
rationale: §59.2/§59.4: a map is `[KeyT: ValT]`; §14.10 supplies the value-type context for a bare variant at a fixed-type position. The VALUE arg of `insert(k, v)` / `getOr(k, default)` is a value of the map's value type, so a bare `.Variant` there resolves against that enum — no E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS.
